rationale: Eight exact official MTA rows establish realized service-start dates, one uniquely anchored current route per row, and fifteen bounded treatment members. Q14, Q51, Q115, and QM65 are atomic service-pattern occurrences. Q61, Q80, Q82, and Q89 are reviewed route-redesign bundles whose analysis family is supported by the official Queens Bus Network Redesign project context. Every predecessor, replacement, and alternative route reference remains treatment text or rider context only; only Q14, Q51, Q61, Q80, Q82, Q89, Q115, and QM65 receive affects_route relations.

clauses.label: Q14 Q23-Q38 segment combination
clauses.source_quote: The new Q14 will combine the Astoria Blvd-Roosevelt Av segment of the current Q23 with the northern half of the existing Q38.
clauses.description: Q14 combined the Astoria Boulevard-Roosevelt Avenue segment of Q23 with the northern half of Q38.

clauses.source_quote: Get more details on Q14 service.
View the new Q14 timetable.

clauses.source_quote: The Q51 will provide a new east-west link along Linden Blvd between Cambria Heights and Ozone Park.
clauses.description: Q51 created a new Linden Boulevard east-west link between Cambria Heights and Ozone Park.

clauses.source_quote: Get more details on Q51 service.

clauses.label: Q61 Beechhurst-Flushing connection
clauses.source_quote: The new Q61 will connect Beechhurst to Flushing, providing service along Willets Point Blvd.
clauses.description: Q61 created a connection between Beechhurst and Flushing via Willets Point Boulevard.

clauses.description: Q61 replaced existing Q15 service in Beechhurst.
